title: scry
description:
Searchable GraphQL ↔ MCP bridge for AI agents.

v0.1 shipped core; v0.2 hardened deployment; v0.3 closed the audit
+ caching loop; v0.4 sharpened dedupe / discoverability; v0.5
hardened the wedge (server coverage 33% → 63.8%, mutation E2E).

v0.6 proves the wedge externally. All five expert lenses
(ai/product/gtm/ux/architecture) converge on one blocker: zero
external users. The product is technically credible but
unvalidated against a real-world schema. v0.6 ships a
reproducible benchmark on GitHub's v4 GraphQL API (~700 types,
public, every reader can repro with a PAT). Measures token
compression, agent task success rate, latency, $$/task — full-
SDL baseline vs. scry-routed agent. The repo becomes self-
marketing: README screenshot IS the distribution asset.

features.title: reproducible benchmark on GitHub v4 schema
features.description:
Ship docs/benchmarks/github-v4/ with everything needed to
reproduce two head-to-head agent task runs.

Task picked for realism (multi-field, joins, requires
picking ~5 types out of 700): "find every PR I opened that
touched file X in the last month, with reviewer + merge
status." Encodes the schema-navigation cost the wedge is
meant to compress.

Two runners:
  - baseline/: paste full GitHub SDL into the agent's
    system prompt, run the task N=10 times.
  - scry/: agent calls schema_search → query_validate →
    query_execute, run the task N=10 times.

Metrics captured per trial: input tokens, output tokens,
wall-clock time, success (boolean — did the agent return
the right answer), $$ cost via published per-million-token
rates. Raw results in results.jsonl; summary table in
README.md.

Single `make bench` reproduces both runs from a clean
checkout given GITHUB_TOKEN + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env vars.

Acceptance: baseline success rate measured; scry success
rate measured; token compression measured against the
"~50× reduction" claim in README.md. Numbers either confirm
the wedge (→ ship the HN/Apollo Slack drop) or refute it
(→ v0.7 plan grounded in observed failure modes, not
speculation).
